Title: Metabolic engineering and mechanical investigation of enhanced plant autoluminescence

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhongyan 100; transgenic lines not individually named
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports luciferin precursor biosynthesis and autoluminescence
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances HispS activity, increases hispidin accumulation, and boosts plant autoluminescence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AnNPGA directly enhanced HispS-mediated conversion of caffeic acid to hispidin in tobacco leaves. 2. Coexpression of NPGA with HispS increased hispidin synthesis about 3-fold over HispS alone. 3. Inclusion of NPGA in the engineered pathway contributed to stronger luminescence in FBP/eFBP plants.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in infiltrated leaves, transgenic tobacco, BY-2 cells and poplar
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; stable transgenic pathway assembly; photon imaging
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sucrose supply; light/dark; DCMU indirectly via pathway flux
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory gene to improve PKS activity and brighten autoluminescent plants without adding a full upstream caffeic acid synthesis module
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts post-translationally to activate polyketide synthase-type enzyme rather than directly catalyzing metabolite formation; codon optimized for tobacco expression

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhongyan 100; transgenic lines not individually named
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/caffeic acid biosynthesis coupled to fungal bioluminescence precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Caffeic acid-forming ferulate/lignin-related branch supplying luciferin precursor biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates p-coumaroyl shikimate/quinate at the 3' position to form caffeoyl shikimate/quinate, thereby increasing caffeic acid/caffeoyl-CoA pool for hispidin production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Hispidin; caffeic acid-driven fungal luciferin precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enables plant autoluminescence and visible illumination; reporter/biosensor potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes caffeic acid and hispidin accumulation and markedly increases autoluminescence intensity in eFBP plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnC3'H1 was selected from multiple plant C3'H homologs as the most effective enzyme for increasing caffeic acid production. 2. Transient expression in tobacco showed BnC3'H1 more efficiently converted p-coumaroyl shikimate toward caffeic acid-related products than other tested C3'Hs. 3. Incorporation of BnC3'H1 into the enhanced fungal bioluminescence pathway increased caffeic acid and hispidin levels and raised light emission to about 3-fold over FBP plants.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in infiltrated leaves, transgenic seedlings, flowers, and BY-2 cells
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane-associated; endoplasmic reticulum inferred for CYP98A-type P450
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic selection; molecular docking; transient overexpression in tobacco leaves; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; stable transgenic expression in tobacco and BY-2 cells; photon imaging
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sucrose supply; light/dark; DCMU; drought; UV; cold; high temperature
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for caffeic acid supply to the FBP, but not definitively established as sole rate-limiting enzyme of native pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering module to boost precursor supply and strengthen autoluminescence in crops and ornamentals; can be stacked with fungal pathway genes for brighter plants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess caffeic acid may cause mild toxicity in BY-2 cells at high exogenous levels; no obvious developmental penalty in tobacco lines reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Four conserved residues Trp112, His242, Trp294 and Thr298 were implicated in substrate binding by docking/modeling; gene function was assessed in heterologous context rather than via knockout of endogenous orthologs

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhongyan 100; transgenic lines not individually named
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Caffeic acid; caffeoyl-CoA-related precursor pool; malonyl-CoA inferred
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of hispidin from caffeic acid-derived precursor substrates in the fungal bioluminescence pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of fungal luciferin enabling autoluminescence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces hispidin and supports plant autoluminescence; activity is strongly enhanced by coexpression of NPGA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HispS alone converted caffeic acid to hispidin in tobacco leaves. 2. Coexpression with AnNPGA increased hispidin production about 3-fold relative to HispS alone. 3. As part of FBP/eFBP constructs, HispS is essential for precursor formation and light output.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in tobacco leaves, stable tobacco lines and BY-2 cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression assay; LC-MS/MS detection of hispidin; stable transgenic pathway assembly
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sucrose supply; light/dark; DCMU indirectly via pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Hispidin biosynthesis described as a bottleneck of the FBP cycle
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core catalytic component for engineering autoluminescent plants; performance improved by pairing with NPGA and precursor-supply genes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dependent on precursor and energy availability; heat sensitivity of downstream light production limits system performance

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhongyan 100; transgenic lines not individually named
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates hispidin to generate the luciferin used by fungal luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required component of the FBP/eFBP enabling autoluminescence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnH3H was included as one of the five fungal bioluminescence genes expressed in tobacco. 2. Comparable expression of the FBP genes was confirmed in FBP and eFBP lines. 3. It functioned as an essential catalytic step in the reconstituted luminescence pathway but was not independently dissected in Results.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in transgenic tobacco and BY-2 cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable pathway reconstruction and luminescence phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required core enzyme for transfer of fungal autoluminescence to plants

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhongyan 100; transgenic lines not individually named
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes fungal luciferin to emit visible green light
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Biophysical emission trait linked to luciferin metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole plant with strong signals in flowers and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates the visible light output of the pathway; heat likely reduces its activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnLuz was one of the five fungal genes stably expressed in FBP/eFBP tobacco lines. 2. Enhanced precursor supply led to higher photons emitted through the same luminescence machinery. 3. High temperature rapidly reduced light despite caffeic acid accumulation, consistent with reduced luciferase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in transgenic tobacco, BY-2 cells and poplar
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable pathway reconstruction; photon imaging under multiple conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential reporter enzyme; future engineering should improve heat stability
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Heat sensitivity limits luminescence under high temperature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhongyan 100; transgenic lines not individually named
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes caffeylpyruvate to regenerate caffeic acid for the luminescence cycle
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for recycling of target-product precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports caffeic acid recycling and sustained autoluminescence in the fungal pathway reconstructed in plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnCPH was incorporated as one of the five fungal pathway genes in FBP/eFBP. 2. Comparable expression of FBP genes was confirmed in transgenic lines. 3. It contributes to pathway recycling but was not independently functionally dissected in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in transgenic tobacco and BY-2 cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable pathway reconstruction and luminescence phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary recycling enzyme for sustainable autoluminescence systems
